title: Why Italians Saw the Pope Very Differently - Ada Palmer
summary: This is a historical commentary by Ada Palmer on how Italian perspectives on papal authority differed from other regions. The discussion explores divergent views and cultural contexts around religious leadership in Italian history.
writeup: Historian Ada Palmer examines how Italians viewed papal authority differently from other regions, drawing on the distinct cultural and historical contexts shaping those perspectives. The conversation centers on why religious leadership was understood in divergent ways across Italian history. The takeaway: perception of the papacy was regionally contingent, with Italian attitudes diverging from those held elsewhere.
